title: DAILIES
description:
In 2020, during the first lockdown, I committed to using the time I’d normally spend commuting to learn 3D. What started as a simple exercise turned into a deep, nearly two-year exploration.
At the beginning, I would spend around 20 minutes modeling something, hit render, and post it—no matter the result. Sometimes it was rushed, sometimes a bit stressful, but it became a study in how to fill a given format with the best of my current knowledge, always learning something new. Often it went beyond 20 minutes, but the outcome was always a “popsicle” — an abstract, sometimes playful or slightly absurd, yet always a kind of floating sculpture.
At some point, it stopped serving its purpose as I shifted focus to more complex processes like animation, and the project became obsolete. Still, the takeaway is simple: if you want to learn something, show up every day, put in the time, and make it count. Over time, it builds into skill, experience, and something genuinely valuable.
